Hero_Section.Main_Title_Line2: Consecrated Women, Living Flames of God's Love

About_Section.Paragraph_1_Lead: The Conference of Religious Women India (CRWI), in collaboration with the Global Research Network (GRN), is delighted to invite you to this Symposium.
About_Section.Paragraph_2: For centuries, consecrated women have embodied Christ’s tenderness through ministries of education, pastoral care, healthcare, formation, social outreach, eldercare, ecological stewardship, and media engagement. Their fidelity and service continue to illuminate challenging realities with courage, creativity, and unwavering hope.
About_Section.Paragraph_3: This Symposium will offer a platform for sharing research insights, personal narratives, and collaborative reflections on the evolving mission of Consecrated Women amid contemporary global changes.
About_Section.Quote_Text: "Consecrated life is a living memory of Jesus’ way of being – a sign that love, when lived radically, can transform the world."

Thematic_Areas.Sub_Theme: Journeying Together in Faith and Renewal
Thematic_Areas.Description: A lifelong pilgrimage of grace calling for spiritual maturity and intercultural dialogue.

Thematic_Areas.Sub_Theme: Seeds of Hope for Future Generations
Thematic_Areas.Description: Shaping conscience, character, and compassion through value-based learning.
Consecrated women as healers and caregivers continuing the compassionate mission of Christ.
Sustainable and faith-rooted eldercare as a moral responsibility and spiritual gift.

Thematic_Areas.Sub_Theme: Witnessing to Christ at the Margins
Thematic_Areas.Description: Advocacy against ecological destruction, displacement, and gender injustice.

Thematic_Areas.Sub_Theme: Radiating Hope in the Digital Age
Thematic_Areas.Description: Creative evangelizers - storytellers of truth, beauty, and hope.

Objectives.Title: Reaffirm Hope
Objectives.Description: Reaffirm consecrated life as a prophetic sign of hope, healing, and ecological stewardship.

Objectives.Title: Foster Dialogue
Objectives.Description: Foster dialogue and participatory research across congregations and disciplines.

Objectives.Title: Integrate Values
Objectives.Description: Integrate Gospel values and synodality into ministries of education, formation, health, and social outreach.

Objectives.Title: Sustainable Strategies
Objectives.Description: Chart practical strategies for sustainable ministries and dignified eldercare.

Objectives.Title: Media Engagement
Objectives.Description: Encourage media engagement and digital presence as new frontiers of evangelization and advocacy.

Leadership_Messages.Name: Sr. Maria Nirmalini AC
Leadership_Messages.Message_Text: "It is with great joy and hope that we welcome you to this symposium. As we gather to reflect on our consecrated life, let us be open to the Spirit's leading, renewed in our commitment to be living flames of God's love in a world that yearns for hope."

Leadership_Messages.Name: Sr. Molly Mathew MSMHC
Leadership_Messages.Message_Text: "This symposium is a milestone in our journey together. It is an opportunity to celebrate our shared mission, to learn from one another, and to chart a path forward that is sustainable, inclusive, and deeply rooted in our charism."

Key_Speakers.Speaker_Role: Deputy Secretary General Of The CBCI

Development Partners.Speaker_Role: Associate Vice President, Program Operations and Head of Catholic Sisters
Senior Program Officer, Catholic Sisters Initiative
Coordinator of Research and Projects- Desarrollo y Salud

Presenters.Paper_Title: Life long formation is a continues renewal of vocation and mission
Presenters.Abstract_Link: A theologian from the Congregation of Teresian Carmelites, Sr. Jamesy has extensive experience in academic formation and holds a Doctorate in Theology. She has taught across major theological institutions and contributes significantly to contemporary discussions on religious and priestly formation.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Mentorship and accompaniment: "warming the dreams of the young" (Pope Francis).
Addressing psychological well-being, intercultural living, and the responsible use of technology
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Dr. Jincy Arakkal George is a psychologist with a specialization in Clinical Counseling, trained at De La Salle University and Loyola School of Theology, Manila, and has served in key leadership roles including Headmistress and Principal.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Intercultural and intergenerational community living
Presenters.Abstract_Link:
Member of the Congregation of the Apostolic Carmel, Sr. Lavina Rodrigues AC is a trained formator with experience accompanying Aspirants, Novices, and Juniors, and holds an MA in English with extensive teaching and formative leadership experience.
Provide your feedback on BizChat

Presenters.Paper_Title: New Education Policy
Reimagining structures to integrate spiritual, human, intellectual, and pastoral dimensions.
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Fr. V. Antony Bhyju, CMF, is a Claretian Missionary with wide experience in initial and ongoing formation, currently serving at the General Curia in Rome as Assistant to the General Consultors for Formation and Spirituality.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Cascading Response and Support Model to Enhance Psychological Integration among Women Religious in India.
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Assistant Professor of Psychology at Christ University, Dr. Patrick specialises in school mental health, stress management, and psychosocial interventions. He has contributed extensively to community‑based programmes, research supervision, and academic training.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Care for the Elderly: Honoring Wisdom Ensuring Dignity
Presenters.Abstract_Link: An educationist with 22+ years of teaching and administration, including roles as Principal and Associate Professor. Her academic work spans ageing, diaspora studies, and community development with contributions to major national seminars

Presenters.Paper_Title: Establishing sustainable and faith -rooted eldercare models that uphold diginity and independence
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Dr. Kripanjali Tellis Nayak is a Registered Nurse and Senior Living Consultant from Mangalore, currently a PhD research scholar in Management, focusing on senior well‑being and the empowerment of young people and women through holistic healthcare and leadership.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Pilgrimage of Care: Evidence-Based Psychosocial Training for Women Religious Serving the Elderly in India.
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Assistant Professor of Psychology at Christ University, she specialises in disability studies, educational psychology, and adolescent mental health. Her clinical background from NIMHANS and work with vulnerable children shape her research and advocacy.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Promoting accessability inclusion and spiritual enrichment in aging communities
Care for the Elderly: Honoring Wisdom, Ensuring Dignity—A Pilot Study on Intercongregational Collaboration in Housing, Healthcare, and Palliative Care
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Sr. Dr. Helen Mary, SABS, is an Obstetrician and Gynecologist with an MBBS and MD from St. John’s Medical College and has served as Chief Medical Officer at Nirmala Health Centre, Bangalore, since 2002.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Effectiveness of Relaxation Therapy on Psychological, Physiological and Bio-Chemical Variables among Elderly Residing at Old Age Homes.
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Assistant Professor of Psychology at Christ University, she specialises in disability studies, educational psychology, and adolescent mental health. Her clinical background from NIMHANS and work with vulnerable children shape her research and advocacy.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Empowering consecrated women as educators and leaders in Christ centered, value-based education.
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Superior General of the Apostolic Carmel Congregation, Sr. Maria Nirmalini AC is President of CRWI and Vice President of CRI, with long experience in education, leadership, and advocacy for gender equality.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Strengthening collaboration between Catholic schools, universities, and congregations for shared mission.
Presenters.Abstract_Link: A scholar in philosophy of education, Fr. Paul researches mission integrity, ethical use of technology, and truth in the post‑truth era. He is a consultant supporting educational and religious institutions and promotes holistic, values‑based education.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Promoting education as a transformative tool for gender equity, ecological consciousness, and social renewal
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Hector Andrade is a philosopher and environmental conservationist who blends ancient wisdom with ecological practice. Formerly an educator, he now focuses on medicinal plant conservation, sustainable living, and ethical education.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Integrating digital literacy, artificial intelligence, and innovative pedagogies with spiritual and ethical formation.
Presenters.Abstract_Link: CEO and Co‑Founder of Eloit Innovations, Dr. Thomson leads global EdTech and Faith‑Tech solutions serving over 700 institutions. Recognised with UN and international awards, he promotes ethical, values‑driven digital transformation in education

Presenters.Paper_Title: Advancing Justice through Education: A Capacity-Building Intervention for Religious Sisters to Promote Child Mental Health in Educational Institutions.
Presenters.Abstract_Link: A mental health practitioner with 8+ years of experience, she has worked with NIMHANS and Carers Worldwide. Her expertise includes child protection, disaster support, domestic violence, and women’s mental health, with over 100 trainings delivered

Presenters.Paper_Title: Pilgrims of Hope in Healing Ministries: The TREASURE Model for Mental Health and Holistic Wellbeing of Women Religious in India
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Senior psychiatrist with over 20 years of experience, Dr. Sally John is Head of the Department of Psychiatry at MGIMS, Sevagram, and founder of Treasure, a national mental health initiative for religious sisters.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Addressing mental health, burnout, and holistic well-being of sisters in healthcare.
Compassionate Healing: Consecrated Women as Healers and Caregivers Continuing the Compassionate Mission of Christ.
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Paediatrician with extensive clinical experience, Dr. Viji Poulose serves as Paediatrician, Sonologist, and Medical Superintendent at Dayasagar Hospital, Amravati.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Training sisters in emerging health technologies, preventive medicine, and compassionate care.
Motivational Factors Influencing Doctors to Provide Holistic Care: A Qualitative study
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Consultant Obstetrician & Gynaecologist at Canossa Hospital with expertise in high‑risk pregnancies, preventive health, and community outreach. A former national leader of Sister Doctors Forum of India, she integrates clinical practice with faith‑based healthcare

Presenters.Paper_Title: Expanding faith-rooted responses to maternal, child, and geriatric health needs.
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Associate Professor of Community Health at St. John’s, Bengaluru, with expertise in gerontology, disaster preparedness, and health systems. He contributes to national health committees and has numerous publications in public health

Presenters.Paper_Title: Empowering Consecrated women as leaders in ecological justice, advocacy, and community development
Presenters.Abstract_Link: A professor of Business Ethics at XLRI and former Principal of Patna Women’s College, Sr. Doris holds doctoral degrees in Clinical Genetics and Zoology. Her expertise spans ethics, sustainability, genetics, and leadership in education

Presenters.Paper_Title: SLYI- A faith based model of skill development, social capital and womens ecnomic empowerment in india
Faith and Freedom: Addressing Human Trafficking at the Margins
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Former Superior General of the Bethany Sisters, Sr. Jyoti Precy Pinto, BS, is Directress of Saanidhya, Bethany Spirituality Centre, and a nationally recognized leader in women’s empowerment and anti‑human trafficking initiatives.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Faith-based responses to systemic injustice
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Grassroots activist and award‑winning journalist, Sr. Sujata Jena is a leading advocate for social justice and human rights among marginalized communities in India.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Pilgrims of Hope at the Margins: Consecrated Women as Defenders of Human Rights and Agents of Justice
Presenters.Abstract_Link: A human rights lawyer practicing across major Indian courts, she specialises in gender justice and institutional compliance. She serves on multiple ICCs and conducts national‑level training in legal advocacy and gender sensitisation.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Grievnce redressal Cell & Safeguarding of minors and vulnerable adults
Presenters.Abstract_Link: A senior human rights practitioner working with grassroots movements, donors, and global platforms. She advocates for social justice, women’s leadership, and marginalised communities, and is known for influential publications and policy engagement.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Radiating Hope: Sacred Presense in virtual spaces
Presenters.Abstract_Link: PhD in Journalism & Communication; Director of MSMHC Communications. She teaches at several universities and leads media strategy within ecclesial communication networks including SIGNIS North East India.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Media Hubs for Inter-Congregational Collaboration: A Communion–Synodal–Network Analysis of Collaborative Digital Mission among Women Religious
Presenters.Abstract_Link: A priest of the Society of St. Paul, Fr. Sajith works in higher education, research, and priestly formation. His academic focus includes spirituality, communication, and pastoral challenges in contemporary society.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Storytelling, visibility, and vocation promotion
Radiating Hope Through the Lens: Reclaiming Women’s Voices and Renewing Consecrated Life in the Digital Age
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Sr. Lismy CMC, of the Congregation of the Mother of Carmel, is a Catholic media missionary from Kerala, popularly known as the “Camera Nun.” She uses filmmaking and digital media for evangelization and social awareness and has produced numerous films highlighting faith, human dignity, and social issues. She is a recipient of the James Alberione Award for her contributions to Christian media.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Media hubs for inter-congregational collaboration: A strategic model for catholic women religious in India.
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Fr. Biju Madathikunnel, C.Ss.R. is a Redemptorist priest and media scholar with over 20 years’ experience in communication and leadership. A former Provincial Superior and communications director in Rome, he integrates creativity, digital media, and intercultural leadership in global contexts.

Presenters.Paper_Title: A Synodal Path toward Authentic Consecrated Living: Perceived Stress and the Role of Social Support & Accompaniment among Perpetually Professed Sisters in India
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Research Coordinator at CRWI, Sr. Vijo Arul Selvi M is a Ph.D. scholar in Mathematics and experienced academic leader, combining international research training with education and spiritual accompaniment.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Moderator
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Fr. Jerin Peter Palatty is a philosopher‑theologian and priest, serving as Head of the Department of Philosophy at Kristu Jyoti College, Bangalore, with advanced studies from Dharmaram Vidya Kshetram and the Pontifical Gregorian University, Rome, and is a published author in philosophy, theology, and spirituality.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Moderator
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Dr. Greta D’Souza is Professor and Chair, School of Education, and Associate Director, Global STEM Education Centre at CHRIST (Deemed to be University), with research expertise in adolescence education and a strong focus on student leadership, communication, and social impact.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Moderator
Moderator
Moderator
Presenters.Abstract_Link: Fr. Joye James SJ is a Jesuit priest and renowned social work educator with extensive leadership experience, currently serving as President of ICTA, Kalamassery, and as a key member of CRWI’s GRC and SRWI initiatives, with long‑standing engagement in social justice and child welfare.

Presenters.Paper_Title: Moderator

Call_For_Papers.Section_Intro: We invite submissions on the thematic areas listed above. Rooted in the spirit of a Pilgrimage of Hope, this gathering seeks to deepen communion and foster renewed commitment.
Call_For_Papers.Guideline_1: Abstracts: 300–400 words (title, theme, objectives, methodology, keywords).

Executive_Council.Name: Sr. Maria Goretti FS
Executive_Council.Congregation: Sisters of Our Lady of Fatima
